description: Two-electron FCI with analytical Slater integrals as functions of orbital exponent k. Three modes: (1) Self-consistent: k^2=-2E (Fock energy-shell). (2) Variational: minimize E(k). (3) Fixed k=Z. Off-diagonal one-body elements (k-Z)*<a|1/r|b> included for k!=Z.

findings.self_consistency_vs_variational: The self-consistency condition k^2=-2E (from Fock projection) gives WORSE energies than variational optimization or fixed k=Z. At n_max=1: SC gives k*=9/4 (E=-2.531 Ha, 12.8%%) vs variational k*=27/16 (E=-2.848 Ha, 1.9%%). The self-consistent energy is algebraically interesting (exact rational at n_max=1) but numerically inferior.
findings.variational_optimized: Variational optimization of k gives remarkably good energies. At n_max=2 (7 configs): 1.6%% error. At n_max=3 (31 configs): further improvement. Variational bound respected (E_var > E_exact at all n_max tested).
findings.matrix_polynomial: H(k) is confirmed as a matrix polynomial in k at all tested n_max (residual < 1e-8). The polynomial degree depends on whether off-diagonal h1 terms are included: with them, H(k) has a k*(k-Z) contribution making it still quadratic in k.
findings.nmax1_exact: At n_max=1 (1s^2 only): self-consistent k* = 9/4 (exact rational), E* = -81/32 (exact rational). Variational k* = 27/16, E* = -729/256.
